mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 210 in CPIAUCSL triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal, or Rank IC > 0.05 in inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market, boosting corporate profits and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect fails to produce positive returns in 70% of cases, or individual series show no directional consistency in labor market signals.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles, indicated by rising FEDFUNDS above 5%, increase borrowing costs and reduce corporate investment, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of high-rate periods, or Rank IC < 0.02 during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong manufacturing activity, increasing corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or INDPRO fails to correlate with SPX returns in 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts (HOUST) below 1,400 thousand units signal weak consumer demand, reducing corporate revenues and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of low-HOUST periods, or Rank IC > 0.04 in demand contraction scenarios.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust demand, boosting corporate profits and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect fails to produce positive returns in 70% of cases, or individual series show no directional consistency in demand signals.

mechanism_narrative: Late-cycle industrial production (INDPRO) growth slows, signaling capacity constraints and inflationary pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of late-cycle INDPRO growth periods, or Rank IC < 0.02 during capacity-constrained phases.
